title: dianthus caesius subsp. furcatus
intro: Dianthus caesius subsp. furcatus, also known as a type of fringed pink, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Dianthus caesius subsp. furcatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
